title: cornus parviflora
intro: Cornus parviflora, also known as the Bitter Flower, is a plant species in the Cornaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. Cornus parviflora is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in herbal supplements to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
